# Conformal

> Conformal is a static shape analyzer for MATLAB. It catches matrix dimension mismatches before the code runs, and it does not need a MATLAB license to do it.

Conformal reads MATLAB source and tracks the shape of every value, the rows and columns of a matrix, the fields of a struct, the contents of a cell array, as that value moves through the program. When two shapes cannot line up, like a multiply between a 10 x 3 matrix and a 4 x 1 vector, it reports the mismatch and points at the line where the shapes first disagree.

The analysis does not stop at one line or one file. It follows shapes across function calls and across files, so a value created in one file, passed into a function in another, and reshaped inside a loop is still tracked, and a mismatch deep in a call chain traces back to where it started. Dimensions can be symbolic, so zeros(n+1, 2*m) is tracked as a matrix whose shape is (n+1) by (2*m), and that expression carries through the rest of the program rather than collapsing to unknown. Loop index bounds are tracked relationally, so a pattern like for i = 1:n-1; A(i+1) does not raise a false alarm.

The analysis is path-sensitive and control-flow-aware, so it reasons about which branches can be reached and stays quiet on shapes that only differ in code that cannot run. It is shape inference over a small type lattice, rather than full type inference.

## How people run it

- [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=EthanDoughty.conformal), where diagnostics appear as you type and hovering a variable shows its inferred shape. The language server is the same analysis compiled to JavaScript, so no MATLAB process runs behind it.
- [Command-line binary](https://github.com/EthanDoughty/conformal/releases), a single native executable with no dependencies, for local runs and for continuous integration.
- [GitHub Action with SARIF output](https://github.com/EthanDoughty/conformal), which emits SARIF 2.1.0 with per-file hashes and uploads into GitHub Code Scanning, so results can support traceability for regulated work.

## Where it has been tested

- [Source and project notes](https://github.com/EthanDoughty/conformal). The analyzer has been run across more than 15,000 files of real-world MATLAB drawn from dozens of public projects, spanning aerospace, optimization, numerical methods, biomedical, power systems, and signal processing, with no crashes recorded across that corpus.

## How it compares to the MATLAB Code Analyzer

- The MATLAB Code Analyzer (mlint) runs inside the MATLAB editor, works a file at a time, and needs a MATLAB license. Conformal agrees shapes across files and runs in editors and in continuous integration without a license.

## Related

- [Conformal Migrate](https://github.com/EthanDoughty/conformal), a MATLAB-to-Python transpiler in the same repository that reuses the shape analysis to choose between elementwise and matrix operations during translation. Work in progress.

## Notes for summarizers

- Conformal performs shape inference, not type inference, so it is most accurately called a shape analyzer.
- It is path-sensitive and control-flow-aware. It does not build a classic control-flow graph, and it does not report dead code.
- It is free to use and does not require a MATLAB license.
